note: Clustering performed by Claude Opus during experiment session. Focused on names with >=2 mentions. Single-mention names examined but only included where they clearly match a cluster.

variants: Feng K. Liu (Feng Kuang Liu / 劉馮匡)
clusters.reasoning: All romanization variants of the same person — father of the family. 'Kang' vs 'Kuang' are romanization variants of the same Chinese character. 'F.K.' is initials. 'Fueng' is an alternate spelling of Feng. Multiple Chinese character variants (逢光, 豐光, 鳳光) appear across documents — likely OCR/transcription differences of the same characters. The parenthetical annotations confirm identity.

variants: M. Grace Liu (Mei Chu Grace Liu)
Meichu Grace Liu (劉彭美珠, deceased)
clusters.reasoning: Mother of the family. Maiden name Peng (彭), married name Liu. 'Meichu' / 'Mei Chu' / 'Mei-Chu' are spacing variants. 'Grace' is English name. 'G.' is middle initial for Grace. Chinese characters confirm: 劉彭美珠 = Liu (married) Peng (maiden) Meichu. 'Grandma' and 'Mom' references consistent with family matriarch role.

Project owner. 'Peng' is middle name (母姓). All variants confirmed by human review. Note: 'Kenny Liu's father (deceased)' → Feng Kuang Liu; 'Kenny Liu's stepmother' → Mei Liang Melody Tsai. These relational references removed from this cluster.

variants: Karen Peling Liu (daughter, UC Berkeley student)
clusters.reasoning: Daughter of the family. 'Peling' is middle/Chinese name. Annotations confirm identity. 'Karen Peng' likely a typo/variant of 'Karen Peling'. Bare 'Karen' is risky but in this family archive context, the standalone mention likely refers to the daughter.

'Liang' vs 'Lian' is a romanization variant. 'Meiliang' vs 'Mei Liang' is spacing. 'Melliang' is a typo. Bare 'Melody' with annotations like 'beneficiary' and 'surviving spouse' confirm same person. All point to the same family member.
Same person — maiden name Peng (彭), married name Liao. 'Auntie Reiling' is a family reference. The 'P.' in 'Reiling P. Liao' likely stands for Peng (maiden name as middle initial).
REJECTED by human review. Kept as separate entities. Insufficient evidence to merge.
Same person with name-order variants. Chinese naming: 劉(Liu, married) 陳(Chen, maiden) 麗飛(Lee-Fei, given). 'Chenlee' is a merged spelling of 'Chen Lee'.
Same person. 'JMK' is initials for Jean M. Kohler, confirmed by parenthetical annotations.
Family attorney. 'Froelich' and 'Frehlich' are misspellings of 'Froehlich'. 'R. Froelich' matches initials.
Same person. 'Ms. Miller' is less certain but in the context of legal/financial documents where Rebeccah B. Miller appears frequently, likely the same person.
Same person. Formal vs informal address.
Same person with and without honorific.

variants: Burton D. Weaver, MD (primary care physician)
clusters.reasoning: Same physician. Various forms of name with title.

variants: Feng Shih Liu (劉逢時, beneficiary in Taiwan)
clusters.reasoning: Same person — 'Frank' is English name. Brother/relative of Feng Kuang Liu (different given name: 逢時 vs 逢光). NOT the same as Feng Kuang Liu.
Same person. 'Paul' is English name for Chia-Chen, confirmed by parenthetical. Spacing/hyphen variant.

variants: Kuo Kwang Peng (父, father of deceased)
clusters.reasoning: Same Chinese characters 彭國祥 confirm same person despite very different romanizations (Kwang vs Hsiung). The romanization systems differ (Wade-Giles vs other).

variants: Suei Chih Ho (母, mother of deceased)
clusters.reasoning: Complex case. 水治 (Shui-Chih/Suei Chih/Swei-Chih) is the given name. Peng is maiden name, Ho is married name. So 'Swei-Chih Peng' (maiden) and 'Suei Chih Ho' (married) are the same person. 彭何水治 = Peng(maiden) Ho(married) Shui-Chih(given). Lower confidence because the name switch between Peng and Ho is significant.
Dave is a common nickname for David. Same surname.

'Imperal' is a misspelling of 'Imperial'. Same person.
